name: Centralised Observability Platform

useCases.use: All production Azure workloads requiring monitoring, alerting, and incident detection
useCases.dontUse: Development and sandbox environments where local logging is sufficient — use the shared non-production workspace instead

useCases.use: Cross-service distributed tracing for microservices architectures running on AKS or Azure Functions
useCases.dontUse: Simple single-service applications where built-in Azure portal metrics are sufficient

useCases.use: SOC 2 and ISO 27001 compliance evidence generation requiring centralised log retention and audit trail
useCases.dontUse: Isolated PoC or innovation projects with no compliance requirements

components: Azure Log Analytics Workspace (centralised) — single hub workspace for all workload log aggregation
Application Insights (workspace-based) — distributed tracing, dependency tracking, and availability tests per workload
Azure Monitor — metric collection, alert rules, and action groups for all Azure resources
Azure Monitor Workbooks — shared operational dashboards for SLA reporting and capacity planning
Microsoft Sentinel — SIEM for security signal correlation, threat detection, and compliance reporting
Azure Monitor Private Link Scope (AMPLS) — private network ingestion for all log sources
Grafana (Azure Managed) — engineering-facing dashboards with Prometheus and Log Analytics data sources
Azure Data Explorer — long-term analytics over historical log data (>90 days hot retention)

guardrails.rule: All production Azure resources must send diagnostic logs to the centralised Log Analytics Workspace — no isolated workload workspaces
Hot retention minimum 90 days; security and compliance logs minimum 2 years; financial audit logs 7 years
All Tier 1 workloads must have P1/P2 alert rules configured with PagerDuty action group
Logs containing PII must be redacted at ingestion via Log Analytics transformation rules before storage
Log Analytics daily ingestion cap must be set per workload; budget alerts at 80% of cap must be configured

composition.relationship: Uses AMPLS for private log ingestion

revisionHistory.author: Alex Thompson
revisionHistory.summary: Initial centralised observability pattern with Log Analytics and Application Insights

revisionHistory.author: Alex Thompson
revisionHistory.summary: Endorsed. Added Azure Managed Grafana, Azure Data Explorer archival, AMPLS private ingestion, and PII transformation guardrail.

strategicIntent: Establish a unified, centralised observability platform across all McCain Azure workloads — aggregating logs, metrics, traces, and security signals into a single pane of glass — enabling rapid incident detection, root cause analysis, and SLA reporting without requiring per-team observability infrastructure.
exceptionProcess: Submit Observability Exception via ServiceNow for workloads requiring isolated Log Analytics workspace. Must include justification (data residency, compliance isolation, or cost isolation requirement), approval from Platform Architecture Board, and quarterly review to assess readiness to consolidate.
interactionSteps: All Azure resources send diagnostic logs and metrics to the centralised Log Analytics Workspace via diagnostic settings — enforced by Azure Policy
Application teams instrument workloads with Application Insights SDK (workspace-based, linked to central workspace) for distributed traces and custom telemetry
Azure Monitor collects platform metrics at 1-minute granularity; alert rules trigger action groups (PagerDuty for P1/P2, email for P3/P4)
Security signals (Azure AD, Defender, network flow logs) route to Microsoft Sentinel via data connectors for SIEM correlation
Workload teams access their telemetry via Azure Monitor Workbooks and Grafana dashboards — scoped to their workload's resource group
Log Analytics hot retention (90 days) rolls to Azure Data Explorer for 2-year warm analytics and 7-year cold archive to ADLS
Monthly SLA reports generated via Azure Monitor Workbooks and distributed to workload owners and CCoE
problemStatement: Without a centralised observability platform, each application team builds isolated monitoring stacks, creating alert fatigue from uncoordinated notifications, inability to perform cross-service root cause analysis, inconsistent SLA reporting, and significant cost duplication from redundant Log Analytics workspaces and Application Insights instances.
securityConsiderations: Log Analytics Workspace must be accessed via Azure Monitor Private Link Scope (AMPLS) — no public internet log ingestion in production
Workload teams must have RBAC-scoped access to their own log data only — no cross-workload log access without explicit approval
Logs containing PII must be transformed at ingestion using Log Analytics transformation rules to redact sensitive fields
Sentinel workspace must be in the centralised security subscription — not the individual workload subscription
Log retention and purge operations must be audit-logged and require CCoE approval for purges outside retention policy
